tobacco.notes: Quit ~13 years ago. Occasional cigar at social events.

alcohol.notes: No history of heavy or daily use; no withdrawal history. No known liver disease.

drugs.details: No illicit drug use. No IV drug use.

occupation.current: Construction foreman (commercial concrete/steel), Local 24 trades, ~20 years
occupation.previous: Started as a laborer at 19; foreman since 2016.
occupation.status: Actively working full-time; physically demanding job.
occupation.exposures: Construction dust (silica, concrete), diesel exhaust; possible incidental asbestos on older renovation sites (no documented heavy exposure). Wears PPE per site rules.

housing.livingWith: Wife Tanya and two children (ages 10 and 13).
housing.notes: Stable housing, strong family support. Wife works as a dental hygienist.

diet.type: Typical American diet; eats out frequently at job sites.
diet.supplements: Occasional protein shakes; a daily multivitamin.

exercise.frequency: Very physically active through work; plays recreational basketball ~weekly.

functionalStatus.iadls: Fully independent. Sole or primary earner for the household.

travel.recent: No long-haul travel or prolonged immobility in the months before presentation.
travel.notes: Relevant because it makes this venous thromboembolism essentially UNPROVOKED — no recent surgery, immobilization, long flights, or known prior clots.

mentalHealth.anxietyScreening: GAD-2 negative; understandably anxious about the new liver finding and the possibility of cancer.
mentalHealth.support: Wife and extended family closely involved. Worried about ability to keep working and provide for the family.

notes: Previously healthy, very active 43-year-old tradesman and primary breadwinner. The venous thromboembolism is essentially unprovoked, which — together with the incidentally discovered large liver mass — raises concern for an occult malignancy-associated hypercoagulable state. His livelihood depends on physical work, which is relevant to shared decisions about anticoagulation duration and any procedure.
